markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Register languages, themes, diagnostics, formatting hooks, and code navigation bridges needed by Handshake. This MT belongs to TiptapMonacoIntegration. Integrate Tiptap and Monaco as the full-featured Handshake editor surface.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Tiptap/ProseMirror is the canonical rich document engine for this WP; Monaco is the canonical code-block/code editing surface embedded inside Handshake.
Tiptap and Monaco must be fully integrated into Handshake packaging, UI state, backend save/load, visual debugging, and tests; no separate external editor app may be required.
scope.expected_behavior: MonacoLanguageRegistration leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: MonacoLanguageRegistration is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified 00efdcd6: language_registry.test.ts 7/7 - Monaco language registration. TiptapMonacoIntegration group complete: full app vitest 60 files/442 tests green; tsc --noEmit + eslint clean; offline Playwright roundtrip PASS (bundled Monaco mounts + round-trips, ZERO external network requests, worker chunks loopback-only). RichTextEditor wired into RichDocumentView over the real rich-doc backend; no src/backend or .GOV product code touched.

findings.title: MT scope ~40% implemented: themes, diagnostics, formatting hooks, code-navigation bridges absent
findings.evidence: MT-166 scope: "Register languages, themes, diagnostics, formatting hooks, and code navigation bridges". On disk only the language registry + detection exists (language_registry.ts). No theme registry, no setModelMarkers diagnostics pipeline, no DocumentFormattingEditProvider, no Definition/Reference/Hover/Completion providers - while the backend code-nav API (knowledge_code_nav.rs: symbols/references/file-lens) is BUILT and waiting (inventory GAP-ED-005). The embedded code editor is not IntelliSense-class.
findings.fix: Iteration item: register Monaco Completion/Hover/Definition/Reference providers backed by /knowledge/code/* (offline, project-aware), a diagnostics source -> setModelMarkers, and a theme bridge to the app theme. Single highest-leverage code-editor gap.
findings.residual: code-intelligence bridge (completion/hover/definition/diagnostics/formatting over /knowledge/code/*) is MT-249's contracted scope per the iteration-3 orders (fixlist item 14 explicitly skipped for this hardening pass)

findings.title: ensureHandshakeLanguagesRegistered is dead code in the product
findings.evidence: grep: only language_registry.test.ts calls it; setup.ts/MonacoCodeBlockView never do. Languages resolve solely because monaco ships these 16 ids; the Handshake aliases/extensions registration this MT names is never executed at runtime.
findings.fix: Call it once from ensureMonacoEnvironment()/createConfiguredEditor (idempotent) or retire the helper and correct the MT handoff wording.

parity_gaps: ED-CODE-INT-003 go-to-definition/references: ABSENT editor-side, backend BUILT (owner MT-166)
ED-CODE-INT-007 semantic tokens: ABSENT (syntax-only highlighting via Monaco built-ins)
missing_tests: completion provider returns project symbols inside a code block (once wired)
diagnostics markers render for a known-bad snippet (once wired)
language registration actually executed at editor mount (currently dead path)

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-166 delivers a curated Handshake code-language registry plus deterministic language detection (fence-info, file-extension, alias normalization) that maps any hint to a canonical Monaco language id with a plaintext fallback. The detection functions are genuinely wired into the live editor pipeline and into the single place that mints the persisted code-block language attr, so the delivered slice is real, complete, and native; broader breadth (themes/diagnostics/code-nav) is explicitly deferred to MT-249 and is not faked here.
findings: app/src/lib/monaco/language_registry.ts:38-55 defines HANDSHAKE_CODE_LANGUAGES (16 curated descriptors with stable ids/aliases/extensions); normalizeLanguageHint (91-96), languageFromFileName (102-111), languageFromFenceInfo (118-122) are pure (monaco-free, jsdom-testable) and deterministic with a never-guess plaintext fallback. This is real, complete logic for the delivered slice, not scaffolding.

Detection is genuinely wired into the live editor/persistence pipeline, not isolated: code_block_serialization.ts:71 makeCodeBlockAttrs uses normalizeLanguageHint as the single canonical normalizer minting the persisted code-block language attr; auto_code_block.ts:37/49/115/127 and MonacoCodeBlockView.tsx:28 also consume the registry. The MT output (the language id) is load-bearing on the persisted code node.

No placeholders/escapes: grep across language_registry.ts, its test, auto_code_block.ts, code_block_serialization.ts returned NO PLACEHOLDER HITS (no todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/'deferred'/'pending MT-'/*Unavailable on the live path). Sub-rule 1 PASS.

Sub-rule 2 is NA at this MT's boundary: MT-166 is pure frontend detection logic; durable PostgreSQL/EventLedger persistence of the code node is MT-168's contracted scope, not this MT. The Handshake-native resource MT-166 touches (Tiptap/Monaco editor pipeline) is exercised by the wired call sites and the 7/7 vitest run (language_registry.test.ts).

Minor aside (not a fail): ensureHandshakeLanguagesRegistered / isLanguageRegistered (language_registry.ts:144,163) are only called from the test (language_registry.test.ts), confirming the adversarial LOW finding. They are a defensive no-op because Monaco already ships these 16 ids natively, so detection works regardless; the live payload is the detection functions, which are wired. Handoff wording could note the helpers are belt-and-suspenders.

The broad scope sentence ('themes, diagnostics, formatting hooks, code navigation bridges') is only ~partially met, but the MT's own adversarial_review_v2 residual and parity_gaps explicitly assign code-intelligence (completion/hover/definition/diagnostics/formatting over /knowledge/code/*) to MT-249's contracted scope. This is documented forward-scope on a named follow-on MT, not fake/placeholder work hidden inside MT-166; the MT's four binding acceptance_criteria (implemented-with-detail, names exact files/APIs, native/no-sidecar, HBR rows) are satisfied.

Boundary/hygiene: commit 00efdcd6 touches only app/src/lib/monaco/language_registry.{ts,test.ts} (+257 lines), within packet allowed_paths (app/src); no /.GOV/, no src/backend, no forbidden SQLite/external-search/Docker. Clean.
evidence_checked: grep of language_registry runtime usages across app/src
grep for placeholders/escapes across MT-166 surface (NO HITS)

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
